rows.canonical: recall_lane_coverage (SENSE 1, selection.lane_coverage) and evidence_lane_coverage (SENSE 2) — two real concepts, keep both names distinct
rows.collision_note: BOTH failure modes. (a) One word, two meanings: retriever 'lane' = QueryPlan recall lane (lane_id, role core/original); chat 'lane' = EvidencePlan side (a comparison side needing min_sources distinct docs). The plan says 'lane coverage' for both. (b) Duplicate implementation: evidence_allocation.lane_coverage (unit-tested) vs chat_orchestrator._evidence_lane_coverage (production) compute the same strong-score/distinct-doc coverage; production never calls the tested one, so tests certify dead code while the live copy drifts (chat's adds doc_names/chunk_ids/best_score). Chat DOES delegate select_lane_support/cap_chunks_per_doc to evidence_allocation (chat_orchestrator.py:64-68, 4422) — coverage should be delegated the same way.
rows.evidence: grep 'lane_coverage(' shows only tests call the pure fn; chat_orchestrator.py:4621,4889 call the inline copy; retriever/__init__.py:3123 comment separates coverage-telemetry from answerability.

Two plan usages are to-be-built policies on this same seam: P1.6 'hydrate child/parent evidence before treating a generated summary as a citation' (checklist:693-694, open) and the owner-architecture deterministic waterfall (full→summary→skip) which supersedes the child_summary HYDRATION_MODE default. Minor (a): ingestion _rehydrate_* is an unrelated deserialization idiom — don't count those call sites when auditing hydration. Build P1.6/waterfall inside hydrate.py's mode dispatch, not as a parallel module (duplicate-implementation risk if a 'summary scaffold hydrator' appears elsewhere).
